title: Magnetic or Non-Magnetic?
activity: Topic M3.S6.T16 — Sorting everyday objects by whether they stick to a magnet (magnetic vs non-magnetic materials)
description: A tap-to-sort Class 6 tool that builds the magnetic/non-magnetic distinction. Twelve everyday object cards — iron nail, aluminium foil, copper coin, plastic ruler, pencil eraser, steel safety pin, rubber band, nickel coin, wooden block, iron key, gold ring, cobalt figurine — are presented one at a time, each resting on a table. For each object the student taps one of two bins: 'Sticks to magnet' or 'Does not stick'. A horseshoe magnet then swoops down over the object: magnetic objects (iron, steel, cobalt) lift off the table and stick to the poles with attraction field lines; non-magnetic objects stay put with a 'no pull' label. A green tick confirms a correct sort; a wrong sort shows a one-line hint about the metal involved (e.g. aluminium and copper are metals but non-magnetic; a 'nickel' coin is really cupro-nickel; gold is non-magnetic). It is a SINGLE guided flow (no separate quiz mode): the student walks all twelve objects with Back/Next (advance gated on answering), then a gamified summary scorecard shows the score (count-up, stars, confetti) and a two-column magnetic / non-magnetic recap with the rule. No drag-drop anywhere — the student only taps one of the two bin buttons.
toolType: tap-to-sort, predict-then-reveal (single flow, no quiz mode)
prodSafe: No 'lucide-react' import — all icons are inline SVGs defined in the file (including a custom horseshoe-magnet glyph), so the tool renders on every iframe-renderer version (an external icon name can never be undefined at runtime).

gradeLevel.description: Tier preset. 6 = heaviest gamification, story framing, biggest text, slowest/most dramatic magnet swoop, 70-particle confetti + 5 stars on the summary. 7 = moderate. 8 = light/formal. Authored for Class 6.

themeColor.description: Accent hint. The tool uses the Singularity palette: header purple, red horseshoe magnet, CTAs orange #FF7212.

showNavigation.description: Show Back/Next between objects (advance is gated on answering each card).

autoPlayDuration.description: Host auto-advance hint, in ms. Ignored — the tool calls setStopAutoNext(true) because every card needs interaction.

objects.description: Which object cards appear and in what order. Pass slugs from the built-in library, a subset to shorten/focus the lesson, or full objects to add/override one: { slug, name, emoji, magnetic: true|false, material, hint }. 'magnetic: true' means it sticks to a magnet (iron/steel/cobalt).

showHints.description: Show the one-line metal hint on a wrong answer (and inline captions).

feedbackMessage.description: Optional one-line coaching message (reserved; the tool already supplies per-card prompts).

objects.name: Iron nail
objects.hint: Iron is magnetic — a nail jumps to a magnet, so it belongs in “sticks”.

objects.name: Aluminium foil
objects.hint: Aluminium is a metal but it is NOT magnetic — foil never sticks to a magnet.

objects.name: Copper coin
objects.hint: Copper is non-magnetic — a magnet has no pull on a copper coin.

objects.name: Plastic ruler
objects.hint: Plastic is not a metal at all, so it cannot be magnetic.

objects.name: Pencil eraser
objects.hint: An eraser is rubber — non-metal, so it never sticks to a magnet.

objects.name: Steel safety pin
objects.hint: Steel is mostly iron, so it IS magnetic — the pin sticks.

objects.name: Rubber band
objects.hint: Rubber is a non-metal — a magnet does nothing to a rubber band.

objects.name: Nickel coin
objects.hint: A real “nickel” coin is a copper-nickel mix, not pure nickel — it does NOT stick to a magnet.

objects.name: Wooden block
objects.hint: Wood is a non-metal, so it can never be magnetic.

objects.name: Iron key
objects.hint: The key is iron — iron is magnetic, so it sticks.

objects.name: Gold ring
objects.hint: Gold is a metal but it is non-magnetic — a gold ring will not stick.

objects.name: Cobalt figurine
objects.hint: Cobalt is one of the few magnetic metals (like iron) — it sticks to a magnet.

answerKey.rationale: Only iron, steel and cobalt are magnetic at this level. Aluminium, copper and gold are metals but non-magnetic. A 'nickel' coin is cupro-nickel (copper+nickel), not pure nickel, so it does not stick. Plastic, rubber and wood are non-metals and never magnetic.

steps.title: Test each object
steps.description: For each of the 12 objects: tap 'Sticks to magnet' or 'Does not stick'. A horseshoe magnet swoops down — magnetic objects lift and stick (field lines), non-magnetic stay put ('no pull'). Correct → green tick + confirmation; wrong → one-line metal hint. Back/Next move between objects (Next gated on answering).

steps.title: Summary scorecard
steps.description: After the last object, a gamified summary: score count-up out of 12, 5 stars + confetti (≥60%), a two-column magnetic / non-magnetic recap with per-object right/wrong marks, the iron-steel-cobalt rule, tier encouragement, and a 'Start over' button.

interactionNotes: TAP-TO-SORT only — two bin buttons ('Sticks to magnet' / 'Does not stick') under each object. NO drag-and-drop of cards (house rule; robust on mobile for young students).

The test is a single responsive SVG side-view scene: the object on a table while a red horseshoe magnet (N/S poles) swoops down. Driven by t (0..1 swoop), the object's magnetic flag, and a revealed flag.

Predict-then-reveal: the student taps an answer first, which triggers the magnet swoop — magnetic objects lift to the poles with dashed attraction field lines; non-magnetic objects stay resting with a 'no pull' tag.

Answer key (curriculum-accurate): magnetic = iron nail, steel safety pin, iron key, cobalt figurine. Everything else is non-magnetic — including the nickel coin (really cupro-nickel) and the gold ring.
Single guided flow — there is NO separate quiz/practice mode and no mode selector. After all 12 objects the student reaches one summary scorecard (score + two-column recap + Start over).
Calls setStopAutoNext(true) and reports setStepDetails on every card change.
Responsive from 320px; touch targets 56px at Class 6; the magnet scene caps at 380px wide and scales with the container.
PROD-SAFE ICONS: no lucide-react dependency — icons are inline SVGs (including the horseshoe magnet), so no 'X is not defined' error regardless of the renderer's injected icon allowlist.
conceptsReinforced: Magnets attract only some materials: iron, steel (mostly iron) and cobalt are magnetic and stick to a magnet.
Most metals are NOT magnetic — aluminium, copper and gold are metals but a magnet has no pull on them.
Non-metals (plastic, rubber, wood) are never magnetic.
Object names can mislead: a 'nickel' coin is usually a copper-nickel alloy, not pure nickel, so it does not stick.
Sticking to a magnet is a simple test that separates magnetic materials from a mixture (links to separation of iron filings with a magnet).
instructions_for_student: For each object, decide whether it sticks to the magnet or not, then watch the magnet come down to test it. Get a green tick when you're right — and a hint about the metal when you're not!
